steps.title: Parabola opens upward and line has positive slope
steps.content: The parabola opens upward, so its leading coefficient must be positive (eliminates A and B, which have $y = -\frac{7}{16}x^2$). The line rises from lower left to upper right, so its slope must be positive (eliminates C, which has $y = -\frac{7}{4}x$). Only choice D has $y = \frac{7}{16}x^2$ with positive coefficient and $y = \frac{7}{4}x$ with positive slope.

steps.title: Verify with the intersection point
steps.content: At the visible intersection point $(4, 7)$ on the graph, test choice D: the parabola gives $y = \frac{7}{16}(4)^2 = \frac{7}{16} \cdot 16 = 7$ and the line gives $y = \frac{7}{4}(4) = 7$. Both equations match this point on the graph. That matches choice D — D is correct.
